Ey, I’m anins1der (

Welcome to my little corner of the internet, where I share my shitty thoughts, ideas and projects.
I am the guy that wrote stuff here (no shit), added a few custom functionalies
and wrote stole some styling from my friends’ blogs that used this very same
theme.
Welcome ↓
About me →
Welcome to blo(c)k
Yep, this was the coolest name I got from my friends when I asked about this
blog’s name uhhuh, best name I got was literally just block
._..
It was actually called “blok”, but uh
I am a Turkish entity who has Turkish “friends”, so considering that and the
Turkish translation, I slightly modified the name and took it
.
Huge thanks to
→ Daudix for this theme.
Seriously, check out his blog, it’s awesome! (and –uh– he has stuff to
read that doesn’t suck, unlike mine :D)
→ Mambuco for being a cool guy and (indirectly)
helping me with this blog (by his repository being on github, and uh also for
being a cool guy in general lol)
→
(…and other) Snug Nook (members)
for being a such big mental help that I could stand on. I know, I didnt really
talk about my mental wellbeing being shit there but hey, THESE PEOPLE ARE
COOL XD
→ Sparrow99tr_ for being a nice guy and approving
everything in place of my nonexistent brain. And (uhh) he’s one of the
“medium content creators”, I’d like to one-up this and making him one of the
“XL-sized content creators” by
subscribing to his YouTube channel
(no, I did not get paid for this. Cmon, now sub to
him-)
→ My friend who suggested the name for this blog (you don’t exist actually, it
was me all along. Guess multi-person disorder really is a thing, huh?)

https://anins1der.com/blog/home/.body:
Over the past few months, I have been pushing myself to do something original for my website; and I have failed miserably.
Like, nginx was pain in the ass, I couldn’t get simple-web-server to work the way I wanted: and the issue was — drum roll — I couldn’t get my website to display nicer links.
Nicer links
What do I mean by “nicer link“s, exactly? You probably seen this before: When you take a look at your URL bar, most sites hide the extension of your page so it looks nicer.
Notice how the URL bar doesn't have an file extensions
…I messed up so bad that I couldn’t do it — like, you had to put .html at the end just to see the contents; and doing that doesn’t trigger page transitions :(
Yep, that’s why I gave up on writing bare HTML and CSS—
New error, “new” setup
If you’ve been following my website lately, chances are you might have seen this layout appear before (along with random nginx brainfarts). As the footer 1 below here says, this website runs on Zola (a simple, but templatable static site generator written in Rust); and on top of that Ametrine lies there.
Ametrine is (not) a complete theme with a medium-sized feature set, designed to work with Zola and it’s limitations.
I’m not exactly the guy that understands things or anything, it’s just that I take readily avaiable stuff from people, modify them a litte bit, “cry” about them not being original and go forward — oh look, I dont know how these got broken, at all!
...how?
Huhh??
…you probably get the idea. I guess it’s time to redo the entirety of the blog from ground up, maybe I’ll clean up every nook and cranny of this thingamabob while I’m at it (lol).
T’is painful.
I took a long time to get stuff back up, not including the time I didn’t do anthing related to this blog (of course I did p r o c r a s t i n a t e)
Hi from a few days later. I finally got my shit back up and running. Everything’s fine. I mean, you can read this in a nice looking theme, so it’s a big pass to me.
It’s time for me to get this boy deployed on my Dokploy stack :)
Well hello there, thanks for taking the quick ride to the footer! ↩
https://anins1der.com/blog/home/.title: Back to Home: Ametrine vs Static File Deployment

https://anins1der.com/blog/restrictions/.body:
Prologue
Once upon a time, where everyone lived freely –excepting the glaring issues–, a
man came to rescue everyone.
That guy’s name was Recep Tayyip Erdoğan.
Now you might ask, “There were”
He became the president 4 times in a row –illegally– now1 (’till today), and
after some stuff happened1; the country was left at a fucked up state that
was surprisingly bearable.
I say was bearable because the change is near.
Age restrictions
Parents love it, we hate it; everyone knows what this dingus is.
The reason we hate it is because it always brings bad stuff to out
side of this table we call freedom. This didn’t change for our
topic, or should i say, probably made it worse.
Reasoning behind it
Children are –well– children, and everyone says that children must get older to start
thinking properly; that ranges from their 4-6. years (start talking, get better
at thinking) to around their 20’s (finish getting better at thinking,
start to gain experience).
So what if we enforce that so no child can get out of this period of theirs? The answer
would be “Do it if you can, don’t do it if not.”.
Unfortunately, sometimes humans just stop thinking reasonably and do random bullshit.
Does this, work?
No. Like, not at all. 99% of the examples I saw were beyond terrible.
This thing, paired with excuse of “child safety”, starts to ruin people’s online identities.
For example, when UK
started to roll out the so-called Kids Online Safety Act,
it started to ruin peoples’ anonymity once mainstream social media platforms started to implement it; some of them
applying it globally (looking at you, Roblox).
Control
When a big country invents a crazy idea, countries copy it. For governments, KOSA
is the golden ticket to gain control over their people. What could go wrong, really?
If anyone asks anything, you just slap a word salad containing “kids, security, online
safety” with some extra spices on top.
If people start to seriously rebel against it, you can just enforce it more because
you can just say “children need safety and we’re just bringing that to you, we
are not trying to control anyone”.
There you go; problem solved, you can now do whatever you want.
The worst part is: This is all done, just to control what and who people can see.
This is a problem. This needs to be solved and giving up your credentials is not the way.
That would be like saying “I don’t have anything to hide”– you do, You’re acting it cool because you want to look cool!
Do you know what could happen if your entire ID card
(or Social Security Card for FREEDOM people)
gets leaked? I don’t know the specifics for your country, but first thing that would happen is
you would shit your pants
because identities are not something you can lose that easily without getting penaltized; and
you know it. You would not give your stuff just to get it stolen, no? Guess what, thousands
of poor souls didn’t think this thoroughly and got their information stolen because the companies
that provide these services did not care enough to delete these data after they’ve been done with
processing your information
–unlike what these shitty, fuckass
corpos said– and to make it worse, these were not even safely storaged — and this
is the reason that these people were shocked to see their shit leaked when they woke up and saw the news.
Epilogue
Restrictions. If there’s a reason for their often negative image, then it must be because of actions like this.
Funnily enough, the main point of “cHiLd SaFeTy” act wasn’t the restriction itself; rather it was the negative
side effects. Negative side effects that are by no means a joke.
These happened for some reasons I cannot disclose here, at least without
getting my ass in jail and ruining my life, unfortunately. ↩ ↩2
https://anins1der.com/blog/restrictions/.title: Restrictions.

Remember the emoji shortcut from Ametrine’s demo?
Apparently it doesnt have any support for local files (yet) as it depends on Mastodon servers’ emojis too.
Since I’m not a Mastodon guy anymore1, I decided to steal rewrite the shortcode (template). I also renamed my template to “sticker“s. so fucking original amirite-
Soo, what we were doing here?
Oh right, listing every custom emoji that’s ever existed like people like to do with their 88x31’s! Right now we have-
2. Yep. Just two fucking emojis:
and
…I’m back with new emojis.
Oh and I’d like to just do this:
Remember that one time where someone made a joke, another guy took it too literally and the first guy responded with the same action? No? I made it happen
(I forget my password and I’m too lazy (and shy) to contact indieweb.social staff about it. Also I’m not really into the whole fedi thing anymore.)

https://anins1der.com/licence/.body:
TL;DR
I am using The Unlicense for my projects, which means you can do whatever you want with the code I write, as long as you don’t try to claim that you wrote it or something like that. You can also use it for commercial purposes, if you want to. I don’t care and give a fuck.
The license says that all of these words below add up to this:
I dedicate any and all copyright interest in this software to the
public domain. I make this dedication for the benefit of the public at
large and to the detriment of my heirs and successors. I intend this
dedication to be an overt act of relinquishment in perpetuity of all
present and future rights to this software under copyright law.
I will give a fuck to the license, and be yapping here for a few words about it; though.
These stuff you’ll see afterwards are directly taken from Unlicense’s website unless stated otherwise. You can make out the stuff that are my words and aren’t anyways .d
What is the Unlicense?
The Unlicense is a template for disclaiming copyright monopoly interest in software you’ve written; in other words, it is a template for dedicating your software to the public domain. (…)
Perfect for my use case.
Why Use the Unlicense?
Because you have more important things to do than enriching lawyers or imposing petty restrictions on users of your code. (…)
I mean, I don’t have enough money, for matter of fact
.
The Unlicense
To opt out of the copyright industry’s game altogether and set your code free, put your next software project into the public domain using the following (un)licensing statement:
In a saner world, you would only need the first one or two paragraphs.
For the time being you’ll probably want to retain the whole shebang.
(You should feel free, though, to leave out the last line containing the link to this site, if that’s your preference.)
https://anins1der.com/licence/.title: License
